value.found_quote: A [`SequentialAgent`](/agents/workflow-agents/sequential-agents/) contains `sub_agents` executed in a fixed order... A [`ParallelAgent`](/agents/workflow-agents/parallel-agents/) runs multiple `sub_agents` concurrently... `SequentialAgent` defines the order... `ParallelAgent` for concurrent execution (Fan-Out).
value.rationale: The page, from Google's Agent Development Kit (ADK) documentation, explicitly describes workflow agents with sequential (SequentialAgent) and parallel (ParallelAgent) execution patterns for sub-agents, with code examples in multiple languages. These are described as pre-built primitives with fixed execution logic (sequential ordering and concurrent fan-out). The page does not explicitly show a dedicated "Loop" agent type by name, but it does cover Sequential and Parallel workflow agents as described in the claim. The claim mentions "Loop" as one of the three types; the page does not mention a LoopAgent specifically, but it does substantiate Sequential and Parallel workflow agents as pre-built template workflow agents providing fixed execution logic structures including sequential and parallel orchestration of sub-agents. The page is marked as supported in ADK across multiple languages and versions, indicating active status.
